title: Washoe County School District Intern Program
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 11, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
DEPT: Community Engagement and Services
SUBJECT: Washoe County School District Internship Program
To advance youth civic engagement and strengthen departmental capacity, the Community
Engagement and Services Department launched a pilot high school internship program in fall
2025 in partnership with the Washoe County School District's (WCSD) Career Technical
Education (CTE) program. Building on the pilot high school internship program's success, positive feedback, and departmental interest
generated, the pilot high school internship program continued into spring 2026 semester.
The pilot high school internship program provides students with hands-on experience in local government while earning
academic credit. While there is no cost to the City, there is an administrative investment related
to collaborating with WCSD, supporting the application process, onboarding, training, and intern
supervision. Based on interest, students are assigned to host departments and supervised by a
designated staff mentor, ensuring structured oversight, accountability, and high-quality learning
experience.
Fall 2025

During the Fall 2025 launch semester, four students were placed across four City departments,
including:
• City Clerk's Office
• Community Engagement and Services
• Parks and Recreation
• Public Safety Dispatch

The four students contributed approximately 240 total internship hours and supported priority departmental
functions including administrative support, constituent communications, supporting front desks,
research, and event coordination. The four students also developed practical skills and gained insight into
public service, contributing to a more engaged and informed community. Key outcomes included
improved turnaround time for routine tasks, increased staff capacity during peak periods, and
exposure for students to public-sector careers. Feedback from both student interns and City staff
was consistently positive, with student interns citing increased confidence and career clarity, and
departments noting the value of added support and fresh perspectives.
Spring 2026
The City welcomed eight students this semester. The eight students are currently serving six departments and
divisions and will provide a total of 480 internship hours this semester. Participating departments
include:
• Arts and Culture
• City Clerk's Office
• Clean and Safe
• Licensing and Code Enforcement
• Parks and Recreation
At the conclusion of the current academic year, Community Engagement and Services Department staff will evaluate the pilot high school internship program, including
available staff capacity, to determine long-term sustainability and possible program continuity.

fields.tldr: The Community Engagement and Services Department launched a pilot high school internship program in fall 2025 in partnership with the Washoe County School District's Career Technical Education program. Four students contributed 240 hours across four City departments in the fall; eight students are contributing 480 hours across six departments in spring 2026. The program provides hands-on local government experience and academic credit at no direct cost to the City, though it requires an administrative investment. Staff will evaluate the program's long-term sustainability at the end of the academic year.
fields.key_stats: Fall 2025: 4 students, 4 departments, ~240 total internship hours. Spring 2026: 8 students, 6 departments, 480 total internship hours projected.
fields.key_decisions: No formal Council decision requested; memo is informational. Staff will evaluate program sustainability and possible continuity at the conclusion of the current academic year.
fields.action_items: Community Engagement and Services Department staff to evaluate the pilot program—including available staff capacity—at the end of the 2025-2026 academic year to determine long-term sustainability and possible program continuity.

projects: Washoe County School District Internship Program
dates: End of current academic year (program evaluation deadline)
people: Cynthia Esparza, Director, Community Engagement and Services
